hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised aggressively before the flop, hoping to build the pot. Your opponent called, showing they were willing to play.
Flop — 7, 10, 10. You bet, trying to take control of the hand, and your opponent called, indicating they might have a draw or a weak hand.
Turn — 5. You bet again, but your opponent raised, suggesting they had improved their hand and were gaining momentum.
River — Ace. When you checked, your opponent bet again, going for value with a made hand.

wrongReasons: 9 6 could play a similar line, but the check on the turn is the tell — that card changes things for the correct hand in a way 9 6 doesn't benefit from.
K A gives you a two pair — a hand most players protect aggressively. The passive moments in this hand don't fit.
With a two pair, 6 7 would likely play faster and bigger. The measured bet sizes in this hand suggest something more modest.
hand.explanation:
Answer: Q 9

Your opponent's call on the flop followed by a raise on the turn indicates they were waiting for the right moment to strike, likely after making a pair. The river bet confirms they had a real hand, and given the board, Q 9 makes sense as they would have made a pair of tens by the flop and continued to value bet afterwards.
